response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with impressive breadth across web, mobile, and systems work, but he does not match this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the mandated 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++. His recent decade has centered on DLang, TypeScript, and Node.js. He has no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or capital-markets systems experience — RIDE Capital was a German fintech for investment administration, not capital markets — and no stated SQL Server or Oracle background. The gap on both mandatory pillars (C++ depth and financial-markets domain) makes him a poor fit despite his general engineering quality.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source tooling authorship
Broad polyglot background with some systems-level work (DLang, C/C++, Java) and DevOps skills
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, dated to 2015 and not Windows-platform focused
No Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. 5-10 year requirement
Missing capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
